results.title: Andrej Karpathy's Keynote & Winner Pitches at UC Berkeley AI Hackathon 2024 Awards Ceremony | Glasp
excerpts:
* 🐕‍🦺 The role of AI in various industries is rapidly evolving, with projects like Dispatch AI showcasing practical applications in emergency services.
* 📽️ The importance of mentorship and resources in hackathons can significantly enhance the quality and impact of participants' projects.
* 🥺 Small projects can often lead to robust solutions if nurtured, as highlighted by the journey of various startups emerging from hackathon experiences.
* 👤 The integration of emotional intelligence technology, such as Hume's EVI, can enhance user experiences in customer service and emergency response sectors.
* 🎁 Companies presented at the hackathon demonstrated a clear trend towards sustainability, social responsibility, and inclusivity, which resonate with current market demands.
* 😒 The use of empirical data to analyze and optimize processes is crucial across various projects, indicating a trend towards data-driven decision-making.

results.title: GitHub - aurelisajuan/TalkTuahBank: 🏅 TalkTuahBank — Overall 1st Place @ HackUTD 2024 & Goldman Sachs Winner | AI-powered, voice-based banking assistant accessible via any phone. Built with Retell AI, OpenAI Swarm, Next.js, Pinata/IPFS, and multilingual support.

Repository files navigation
    * README

TalkTuahBank
Multi-Agent, Conversational AI, & Telephony Integration
Frontend built with:
Backend built with:
🏅 HackUTD - Overall 1st Place & 1st Place Sponsor Challenge: Goldman Sachs
Inspiration
Access to banking services is a fundamental aspect of economic participation, yet over 1.7 billion adults worldwide remain unbanked. Traditional banking solutions often require internet access, digital literacy, or proximity to physical branches—barriers that leave underserved populations without essential financial tools.
Inspired by the need for inclusivity and the power of conversational AI, we envisioned TalkTuahBank, a voice-based banking assistant that democratizes financial services by making them accessible to everyone, regardless of their technological capabilities or geographical location.
What It Does
TalkTuahBank revolutionizes the banking experience by offering a fully conversational AI assistant accessible through simple phone calls. Users can effortlessly manage their finances by performing tasks such as checking account balances, transferring funds, and paying bills using natural voice commands.
Key features include:
    * Voice-Activated Services : Conduct banking transactions hands-free via phone.
    * Multi-Language Support : Interact in multiple languages and dialects to accommodate diverse users.
    * Accessibility : Operates on any phone without the need for internet or smartphones.
* AI Assistance : Receive personalized financial advice and support through intelligent interactions.

How We Built
We developed TalkTuahBank by integrating cutting-edge technologies to create a seamless and secure telephonic banking experience:
    * Conversational AI : Utilized Retell AI for natural language understanding and generation, enabling smooth voice interactions.
    * Multi-Agent Framework : Implemented OpenAI Swarm for agent orchestration to manage complex dialogues and backend processes.
    * Secure Data Storage : Leveraged Pinata (IPFS) for decentralized and secure storage of user data and transaction records.
    * Admin Dashboard : Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, ShadCN UI, and Aeternity to provide a robust interface for system management, transaction monitoring, and user support.
    * Telephony Integration : Employed Retell API for handling voice calls and processing voice recognition.

Challenges We Ran Into

#### About the challenge

BatchHacks is a hackathaon for students. It is an open ended hackathon. Participants are welcome to submit anything code related that they would like, from ML to Data Visualization to games.

By joining this hackathon, you consent to receiving emails about the hackathon in your inbox.

404 Found is sponsored and hosted by Batch 09.

### Requirements

#### What to Build

Anything!

1. GitHub Repository:
   
    + Share a link to your GitHub repository containing all the source code, documentation, and any other relevant materials. Ensure your code is well-documented to help judges understand your implementation.
2. Project demo link, if applicable. Every project should submit a github link. Include specific instructions for how to use the project.
3. Submission Video:  
    + You must include a video (up to 3 minutes) demonstrating your project.
This video should walk through your project, highlighting its features, functionality, and user interface. Mention the technologies used and how it addresses the topic of social good.
    + Upload the video to a platform like YouTube or Vimeo and provide the link in your submission.

results.title: Quick Emergency Responses with AI: Developer Spotlight
excerpts:
In just 27 hours during the UC Berkeley's AI hackathon, Bill and his team integrated AI agents, LLM evaluations, and physical phone calling into a comprehensive SaaS platform. Dispatch AI processes emergency calls through Twilio\*, utilizing a custom Mistral AI LLM fine-tuned on Intel Tiber Developer Cloud to generate responses. Simultaneously, the system employs OpenAI\* GPT-4 to extract critical information from call transcripts. The team also incorporated emotion detection feature into the solution for more empathetic call responses.

The interactive UI of Dispatch AI looks as follows:

**Fig.1:** User interface of Dispatch AI

### **Architecture of Dispatch AI**

Following is a comprehensive architecture design of Dispatch AI that helps visualize the communication flow across various components of the hardware and software stack used in the project:

**Fig.2:** Communication flow across Dispatch AI’s architecture

### **Intel’s AI Tools and Technologies Used**
Bill and his team leveraged comprehensive Jupyter Notebooks readily available on Intel Tiber Developer Cloud throughout the development of the project. They also used [Intel® Extension for PyTorch\*](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/articles/technical/accelerate-with-intel-extension-for-pytorch.html) for model optimization.

### **Results Achieved Using Intel’s AI Developer Resources**

The resources utilized on Intel Tiber Developer Cloud helped achieve a remarkable reduction in inference time from 2 minutes 53 seconds to less than 10 seconds. Nearly 80% reduction in the processing time was attained using Intel’s powerful and optimized AI software.[1]

### **Impact and Recognition**

Bill and his team won the first-place prize in the ‘ **Best Use of Intel AI** ’ track and a grand prize in the ‘ **Best AI for Good** ’ category at the hackathon, securing over $50,000 in prizes and investments.
Each of the team members was also awarded a Lenovo\* AI PC by team Intel. This achievement highlights the potential of Intel Tiber Developer Cloud in developing cutting-edge AI solutions for critical real-world problems.

### **Scope for Future Enhancement** **of Dispatch AI**

The success of Dispatch AI has garnered interest from police departments around California, prompting Bill and his team to consider turning their project into a startup. If they proceed with the startup, they are planning to fine-tune the model on [Intel® Gaudi® 2](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/ai-accelerators/gaudi2.html) AI accelerator GPU, further optimizing the solution for speed and latency.

|Check out **the complete project details of Dispatch AI** [here](https://devpost.com/software/dispatch-ai) . |
| --- |

### **What’s Next?**

Sign up to Intel Tiber Developer Cloud today and get started with accelerated development of AI, HPC and edge-computing solutions!

[OpenAI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?organization=OpenAI)

[Managed by Devpost](https://devpost.com/hackathons?managed_by_devpost_badge=1) [](https://help.devpost.com/article/42-what-does-e2809cmanaged-by-devposte2809d-mean?utm_source=devpost&utm_medium=hackathon&utm_campaign=managed)

[IoT](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=IoT) [Machine Learning/AI](https://devpost.com/hackathons?
themes%5B%5D=Machine+Learning%2FAI) [Robotic Process Automation](https://devpost.com/hackathons?themes%5B%5D=Robotic+Process+Automation)

gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b are here. Developed with feedback from the open-source community, these models push the frontier of open models at their size.

Hugging Face, NVIDIA, Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio and OpenAI have come together to host this six week hackathon. Your challenge is to apply these models in creative, unexpected ways: build a robot with reasoning, craft a local agent that thrives offline, fine-tune a model into something indispensable, or push the edge with the weirdest hardware you can imagine. Maybe your project will benefit humanity, or maybe it’ll be so unexpected it surprises everyone in the room. However you build, this is your moment to redefine what open models can do.

##### Get Started

* Both gpt-oss models are [free to download on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/collections/openai/gpt-oss-68911959590a1634ba11c7a4) .

results.title: 2026 Startup Week Buildathon: Tech@NYU's Premier Buildathon. Real Products. Real Opportunities. - Devpost
excerpts:
Rolling Applications will follow. All decisions finalized by February 16th 11:59PM EST.

Please complete application by visiting the [Google Form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSctn9e7Uj08mJjmy_72UZYutnEfI2RuzEhfUWX0S9RUdssOVQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) on our [website](https://nyusw.com/buildathon) . Additionally, check out the FAQs to see if we have answered potential questions you may have. If you have more questions, please email us at startupweek@techatnyu.org .

### Requirements

You must opt in to **one and only one track** . You can opt into as many sponsor/category prizes as you wish (provided you follow any listed guidelines). There is a maximum of 4 people per team. Everyone will be automatically competing for the "Best Overall" prize.

Submit everything by **Sunday, Feb 22nd at 12PM ET** . You are only allowed to submit one entry. If you are using existing code from prior to this hackathon, you must disclose that to the judges.
Only the code you developed this weekend will be considered for judging purposes. No cross-posting is allowed. You must be present at the closing ceremony to claim your prize.

Your project should have some level of functionality and should attempt to solve the problem that is proposed by the track you have chosen.

Submit a link to your project as a working demo and/or a GitHub repository or zip file. Make sure to add " http:// " or " https:// " before your URL (otherwise we can’t see it!). Also, be prepared to record and upload a video showcasing your work.

### Hackathon Sponsors

### Prizes

$ 21,300 in prizes

###### Best Overall Hack (1st Place)

$ 1,500 in cash

1 winner

1st Place:  
1x Nvidia RTX 5080  
Per Team member (Eleven Labs Pro Tier - $99 Per Month)  
Exclusive Dinner w HOF Capital & Head of Innovation @ Nvidia

###### Best Consumer Track

$ 500 in cash

3 winners

1st Place:  
$500 Visa Gift Card  
Interview with Vert for FT or Internship

2nd & 3rd Place:
Interview with Vert for FT or Internship

###### Best AI Automation Track

$ 500 in cash

3 winners

1st Place:  
$500 Visa Gift Card  
Interview with August Law for FT or Internship

2nd & 3rd Place  
Interview with August Law for FT or Internship

###### Best FinTech Track

$ 500 in cash

$500 Visa Gift Card

2nd & 3rd Place  
Interview with TrueMarkets for FT or Internship

###### Best use of Databricks

$ 3,000 in cash

$1000 Databricks Credits

###### Best use of Lovable
